time_tracking_apis.name: Toggl Track
time_tracking_apis.rate_limits: 1 request per second (Leaky bucket); Sliding window quotas: Free (30/hr), Starter (240/hr), Premium (600/hr) per user/organization.
time_tracking_apis.auth_notes: Uses HTTP Basic Auth. The API token serves as the username, and the string 'api_token' is used as the password. A 'User-Agent' header is required for all requests.

time_tracking_apis.name: Harvest
time_tracking_apis.rate_limits: 100 requests per 15 seconds for general API; 100 requests per 15 minutes for Reports API.
time_tracking_apis.auth_notes: Supports OAuth 2.0 and Personal Access Tokens. Requests must include 'Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>', 'Harvest-Account-Id: <ACCOUNT_ID>', and a descriptive 'User-Agent' header.

time_tracking_apis.name: Clockify
time_tracking_apis.rate_limits: 50 requests per second when using X-Addon-Token; approximately 5-10 requests per second recommended for X-Api-Key to avoid 429 errors.
time_tracking_apis.auth_notes: Authentication is handled via the 'X-Api-Key' or 'X-Addon-Token' headers. API keys are generated in the user's Profile Settings.

time_tracking_apis.name: Everhour
time_tracking_apis.rate_limits: 20 requests per 10 seconds per API key.
time_tracking_apis.auth_notes: Requires the 'X-Api-Key' header. Users can find their API key at the bottom of their profile page. The API is currently in beta and accepts only JSON content.

time_tracking_apis.name: 7shifts
time_tracking_apis.rate_limits: 10 requests per second per Access Token across all endpoints.
time_tracking_apis.auth_notes: Uses OAuth 2.0 for partners or long-lived Access Tokens for personal/internal usage. Access Tokens provide administrator-level authorization by default.

effort_recommendation.rationale: No native node - requires HTTP Request configuration
